content: The 2024-2025 cutting-edge evolution of active learning agent memory, which centers on differentiating high-information, curiosity-prioritized training samples from noise for episodic planning, can be reframed as a collective population property rather than just an individual agent trait: a swarm of simple low-memory agents can collectively approximate the SOTA performance of a single large active memory agent by distributing memory storage across the population, where each agent only actively learns and retains the rare high-information samples it encounters. This distributed approach leverages the same curiosity-driven sample selection core of individual active memory to avoid collective information bloat.
